regime: Risk-Off Reversal (Equities Flip Short, Crude Spikes)
summary: A near-mirror of Monday. One session after equities flipped long, the risk-on unwound: MNQ lost its 29,802 stop and 2-closed short (-1.62%), ES flipped short by a whisker (-0.07%), and gold kept falling with no haven bid (-1.71%). The standout was crude, +4.96% and flipping long, a crude-led inflation impulse that lifted real-yield fear into tomorrow's FOMC minutes. The book stays flat and DEFENSIVE; the trend edge is short but a 14:00 ET release argues against full size on a one-day reversal.

keyLevels.label: MNQ ATR Stop
keyLevels.note: Flipped SHORT; price -187 below. The line for the reversal short.

keyLevels.label: MNQ AVWAP / RTH Low
keyLevels.note: Closed on VWAP; 29,210 is the shelf, 29,000 psych below.

keyLevels.label: MES ATR Stop / RTH Low
keyLevels.note: Flipped short by a whisker; 7,530 still defends the record.

keyLevels.label: MGC ATR Stop
keyLevels.note: Short and falling; no haven bid, price -40 below.

keyLevels.label: MCL ATR Stop / psych
keyLevels.note: Flipped LONG; +1.35 above. Energy leadership into the minutes.

setups.name: TSMOM-20 1hr (short)
setups.entry: Short continuation on failed retests of the 29,421 VWAP; not at the 29,210 low
setups.bias: IN-HABITAT short. Reduce size into the 14:00 ET FOMC minutes.

setups.name: AVWAP Deviation Band (MGC)
setups.entry: Reversion long only on a reclaim of the -2s 4,141 band with an exhaustion bar
setups.bias: BORDERLINE. Half size; reclaim required, no knife-catching.

event.name: No scheduled Tier-1 US release (FOMC June minutes Wed Jul 8 14:00 ET)
event.actual: Light US calendar. The tape was driven by a crude oil surge (+4.96%) and a tech-led equity de-risk (MNQ -1.62%, MES -0.07%), with gold selling off (-1.71%) despite the risk-off, an unusual no-haven-bid signal.
event.nqReaction: MNQ flipped its 1hr stop short, 2-closing below 29,802 and running to a 29,210 low before settling on its 29,421 VWAP.
event.crossAsset: Crude led (+4.96%, flipped long); gold fell (-1.71%, no haven bid); ES outperformed NQ. Reads as a crude-led inflation impulse lifting real-yield fear into the FOMC minutes.

observe.name: GAP_NQ (gap fade)
observe.note: A large risk-off day sets up a possible Wednesday gap; watch, no clean fade yet.

observe.name: TSMOM252LO_NQ (long-only 252)
observe.note: The longer-horizon long is now pressured by the short-horizon flip short; a base test into the FOMC minutes.

observe.name: ES confirmation
observe.note: MES flipped short with NQ, aligning the trend edge down, but ES fell only 0.07% so the break is fragile and NQ-concentrated.

risk.throttleState: REDUCED to DEFENSIVE. The trend edge flipped short, but expanding volatility and the 14:00 ET FOMC minutes tomorrow argue against full size on a one-day reversal.
risk.ddProximity: Not binding; the book entered flat and the full daily budget is intact.
risk.sizing: A TSMOM-20 short is in-habitat but a starter only into the event, added on failed VWAP retests not at the 29,210 low. The gold short is late (40 points below its stop) and the crude long chases a +5% day.
risk.note: Week ahead: FOMC June minutes Wed Jul 8 14:00 ET; CPI (June) next week. Treat Wednesday's 14:00 ET release like an event open, first breaks after it are suspect.

psychology: Yesterday's reversal is not today's invitation to chase. NQ flipped short one session after flipping long, and the pull is to trust the newest move at full size. Whipsaw tape punishes conviction. The crude spike and the no-haven-bid gold selloff both say positioning is unstable heading into the FOMC minutes. Let the 29,606 stop and the 14:00 ET release define the terms. A 2-close, not a first touch, earns the size.

DirectionRISK-OFF REVERSALHighEquities flipped SHORT (MNQ, MES); crude flipped LONG; gold stayed short. Near-mirror of the Jul 6 risk-on.
VolatilityEXPANDINGMedium-HighMNQ ATR14 123 (from 108); MES 14.7 (from 12.4). Crude range exploded on a +4.96% day.
SessionPRE-EVENT (FOMC minutes eve)HighLight US calendar today; FOMC June minutes Wed Jul 8 14:00 ET next.
InventoryTrapped equity longs / momentum-long crudeMediumJul 6 risk-on buyers underwater; energy squeezed higher.
EventPRE_EVENTMediumNo tier-1 US release today; a crude-led inflation impulse drove the tape.
LiquidityNormalMediumFull depth, no holiday distortion.

yesterdaysMapScore.note: Graded the Jul 6 map (targeting Jul 7). Row 2 TRIGGERED and PLAYED OUT (MNQ 2-closed below 29,802, ran to 29,210, fresh long voided). Rows 1, 3, 4, 5 NOT TRIGGERED, including MCL which did the opposite and flipped long on a +4.96% day.
